evidence: All public types use PascalCase class names, camelCase method/variable names throughout — no naming-convention drift

API layer consistently uses `handle*` prefix for HTTP handler methods: handleSketchRequest, handleSemanticFieldExplore, handleSemanticFieldExploreMulti, handleSemanticFieldComparison, handleSemanticFieldExamples, handleBlackLabIndexCommand, handleBlackLabQueryCommand, handleBlackLabServerCommand

Handler file split: SketchHandlers and ExplorationHandlers follow identical structural patterns — package-private class, private static Logger, private final fields, constructor, public void handle* methods, private helpers. Consistent across both files.

model/ mixes record types (AdjectiveProfile, CoreCollocate, DiscoveredNoun, RelationType, PosGroup) and traditional classes (ExplorationResult, ComparisonResult, QueryResults) — this asymmetry is explicitly documented in package-info.java with rationale
Static utility classes (HttpApiUtils, RelationUtils, CqlUtils, LogDiceCalculator) all use the same pattern: private constructor, all-static methods, no state — consistent style
config/ has two distinct styles: GrammarConfigLoader (static-method factory) and GrammarConfig/RelationConfig (data objects) — appropriate difference given their distinct roles

CollocateQueryHelper in query/ vs CollocateProfileComparator in exploration/ — both are named with the Helper/Comparator functional suffix but are in different packages, which is semantically correct: they help different subsystems
ExploreOptions (exploration/) vs ExplorationResult (model/) — the Explore/Exploration naming asymmetry is documented in ExploreOptions.java Javadoc as intentional
Private nested record ExecutedSketch in SketchHandlers.java is the only example of a private nested type across the codebase — minor structural outlier but scoped appropriately
BlackLabQueryExecutor and CollocateQueryHelper both use final fields set in constructor — consistent constructor-injection pattern
convention_outlier.issues_preventing_higher_score: The model/ package's mixed record/class style, while documented, still creates a non-obvious navigational pattern for new contributors. The ExploreOptions.includeExamples field is declared but never used anywhere in production code, which is an outlier in an otherwise used-everywhere options record.

strengths: Naming conventions are uniformly followed: no snake_case methods, no inconsistent capitalisation, no style islands in any package
The `handle*` prefix is used consistently across all HTTP handler methods in all handler classes
Static utility class pattern (private constructor, all-static) is applied identically across HttpApiUtils, RelationUtils, CqlUtils, LogDiceCalculator
Mixed record/class style in model/ is explicitly documented in package-info.java, converting a potential surprise into a communicated decision
convention_outlier.issue_character: Outliers are narrow and isolated: one unused record field (includeExamples), one undocumented naming asymmetry (Explore vs Exploration), and one private nested type that breaks the one-class-per-file norm — none systemic.
convention_outlier.score_rationale: The codebase achieves strong naming consistency across all packages: Java conventions, handler prefix, utility-class shape, and constructor-injection are all uniformly applied. No package deviates from the rest's structural norms. The documented model/ style mix and the noted Explore/Exploration asymmetry both carry explicit justification. What prevents a higher score is the unused includeExamples field in ExploreOptions (a dangling contract in an otherwise precise codebase) and the single private-nested-type outlier (ExecutedSketch). These are minor but genuine violations of the otherwise consistent pattern.

issues.summary: ExploreOptions.includeExamples is declared and accepted in all four exploration handler call sites but is never read by SemanticFieldExplorer or any other caller
evidence: ExplorationHandlers constructs: 'new ExploreOptions(topCollocates, nounsPerSeed, minLogDice, minShared, false)' — always false
SemanticFieldExplorer.exploreByPattern accepts ExploreOptions but never references opts.includeExamples()
No other caller reads the includeExamples field
issues.suggestion: Remove includeExamples from ExploreOptions if the feature is not yet implemented, or add a // TODO comment documenting when it will be used and what it should do. Leaving a parameter that is always false and never read creates a false contract.

root_causes: includeExamples was added to ExploreOptions anticipating a feature that was not completed; the field was never cleaned up after it became dead

The record/class mix in model/ reflects genuine evolution (records added for new types, legacy types kept as classes) rather than inconsistency — acceptable but requires documentation which is present
likely_symptoms: includeExamples is a symptom-level dead-field within the broader ai_generated_debt cluster
possible_false_positives: The Explore/Exploration naming asymmetry is documented as intentional — not a convention defect
The mixed record/class style in model/ is documented and intentional
